test_cases.test_case: Successful login with required fields filled (Remember Me optional)
preconditions: User not authenticated, acting as <role>
Login page is open
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Check the Remember Me checkbox
4. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: redirects to Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to Forgot Password page via link
preconditions: User not authenticated, acting as <role>
Login page is open
steps: 1. Click the 'Forgot Password?' link located below the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: navigates to Forgot Password page

test_cases.test_case: Username required - leave blank while other required fields filled
preconditions: User is on the Login page
User is not authenticated
steps: 1. Leave the Username field blank
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Username field displays an inline validation error indicating it is required; the Login button remains disabled and the form does not submit; user stays on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Password required - leave blank while other required fields filled
preconditions: User is on the Login page
User is not authenticated
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Leave the Password field blank
3. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Password field displays an inline validation error indicating it is required; the Login button remains disabled and the form does not submit; user stays on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: All required fields empty - attempt submit
preconditions: User is on the Login page
User is not authenticated
steps: 1. Leave the Username field blank
2. Leave the Password field blank
3. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Both Username and Password fields display inline validation errors indicating they are required; the Login button remains disabled and the form does not submit; user stays on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Invalid credentials supplied while both required fields filled
preconditions: User is on the Login page
User is not authenticated
steps: 1. Enter <username> in the Username field
2. Enter <incorrect password> in the Password field
3. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: A visible error message appears indicating an invalid credentials error message (login failure); the form does not redirect to the Dashboard and the user remains on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Very long Username input (200+ chars) retained and does not truncate
preconditions: Browser on Login page
steps: 1. Focus the Username field
2. Enter a string of 200+ characters into the Username field
3. Move focus out of the Username field (blur)
4. Enter any non-empty value into the Password field
5. Observe the Login button enabled/disabled state
test_cases.expected_result: Username field displays and retains the full entered 200+ character string (no visible truncation). No inline validation message about length is shown. Login button becomes enabled after Password is non-empty.

test_cases.test_case: Password accepts emoji and special-unicode characters (masked) without inline validation
preconditions: Browser on Login page
steps: 1. Enter any non-empty value into the Username field
2. Focus the Password field
3. Enter a sequence of emoji and special/unicode characters into the Password field
4. Move focus out of the Password field (blur)
5. Observe the Password field masking and the Login button enabled/disabled state
test_cases.expected_result: Password field accepts emoji and special/unicode characters (characters are masked). No inline validation error is shown for character content. Login button becomes enabled when Username and Password are both non-empty.

test_cases.test_case: Leading and trailing whitespace in Username is trimmed on blur (not treated as empty)
preconditions: Browser on Login page
steps: 1. Enter a Username value that has leading and trailing whitespace surrounding non-empty text into the Username field
2. Move focus out of the Username field (blur)
3. Observe the displayed value in the Username field
4. Enter any non-empty value into the Password field
test_cases.expected_result: On blur the Username field visually trims leading and trailing whitespace (displayed value no longer has leading/trailing spaces). The field is not treated as empty and no inline 'required' validation is shown. Login button becomes enabled when Password is non-empty.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid back-navigation after successful login: Login form shown blank and not pre-filled
preconditions: A test account with valid credentials exists
Browser on Login page
steps: 1. Enter valid Username into the Username field
2. Enter valid Password into the Password field
3. Click the Login button
4. Wait for redirect to Dashboard (on_success)
5. Use the browser Back button once
6. Observe the Login page and the Username and Password fields and the Login button state
test_cases.expected_result: After pressing Back the Login page is displayed with Username and Password fields empty (no credentials pre-filled) and the Login button disabled. The form does not auto-submit; user must re-enter credentials to attempt login again.

test_cases.test_case: Search recent activities filters the Recent Activities list
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Home Page
2. Enter <search term> in the Search Activity field
3. Submit the search (press Enter or click the search control)
test_cases.expected_result: Recent system activities list is filtered to show only results matching <search term> (filters recent system activities)

test_cases.test_case: Clicking Dashboard navigates to the Dashboard view
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Home Page
2. Click the Dashboard button
test_cases.expected_result: Dashboard view is displayed (navigates to Dashboard view)

test_cases.test_case: Home Page displays welcome message and system version information
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Home Page
test_cases.expected_result: "Welcome, mifos!" message is visible in the welcome card and system version information for Mifos and Fineract is displayed at the bottom of the page

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot access Home Page (redirect to login)
preconditions: User is not logged in / unauthenticated
steps: 1. As an unauthenticated user, navigate to the Home Page URL
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation is blocked: the Login page is displayed and the Home Page content is not shown. Specifically, the welcome card ('Welcome, mifos!'), the Search Activity input, and the Dashboard button are not visible.

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot open Dashboard (direct navigation blocked)
preconditions: User is not logged in / unauthenticated
steps: 1. As an unauthenticated user, directly navigate to the Dashboard view URL or attempt to trigger the Dashboard action without logging in
test_cases.expected_result: Action is blocked: the Login page is displayed and the Dashboard view is not shown. No Dashboard content is visible and the user is not navigated to the Dashboard.

test_cases.test_case: Very long search query in Search_Activity
preconditions: user must be logged in
Home page is displayed with Search_Activity input visible
steps: 1. Focus the Search_Activity input
2. Type a very long string (200+ characters) into Search_Activity
3. Press Enter or click the Search button to submit the search
test_cases.expected_result: Search submission succeeds: the results area updates to show filtered activities. The Search_Activity input either displays the full entered query (if accepted) or displays a visibly truncated value (if UI enforces a length limit); in both cases the results correspond to the visible query and the UI does not crash or freeze.

test_cases.test_case: Special characters and emoji in Search_Activity
preconditions: user must be logged in
Home page is displayed with Search_Activity input visible
steps: 1. Focus the Search_Activity input
2. Enter a search string containing a mix of special characters and emoji into Search_Activity
3. Press Enter or click the Search button to submit the search
test_cases.expected_result: Search submission succeeds: the results area updates without UI errors. The Search_Activity input displays the entered characters if allowed or a sanitized version if the UI strips unsafe characters; either way the UI remains responsive and shows filtered results (no crash or unhandled error message shown).

test_cases.test_case: Leading and trailing whitespace in Search_Activity is trimmed
preconditions: user must be logged in
Home page is displayed with Search_Activity input visible
steps: 1. Focus the Search_Activity input
2. Enter a valid search term with leading and trailing spaces into Search_Activity
3. Press Enter or click the Search button to submit the search
test_cases.expected_result: Search submission succeeds: the application trims leading and trailing whitespace from the query before filtering. The Search_Activity input (after submit or on the results view) displays the trimmed value (no leading/trailing spaces) and the results correspond to the trimmed query.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of Dashboard button
preconditions: user must be logged in
Home page is displayed with Dashboard button visible
steps: 1. Ensure the Home page is visible
2. Click the Dashboard button once
3. Immediately click the Dashboard button a second time (rapid double-click)
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation to Dashboard succeeds once: the application navigates to the Dashboard view and the second rapid click is ignored (no duplicate navigation or duplicate UI artifacts). The user ends up on the Dashboard view and only a single Dashboard view navigation is recorded in the UI (no error dialog shown).

test_cases.test_case: Open Dashboard from Home page and verify main elements
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
on Home page
steps: 1. Click the 'Dashboard' button on the Home page
test_cases.expected_result: navigates to Dashboard page; Dashboard page displays the Search Activity field at the top and the 'Client Trends' chart with legend items 'New Clients' and 'Closed Clients'

test_cases.test_case: Client Trends chart shows both series and legends for a selected office
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
Dashboard page is open with <selected_office> selected
steps: 1. Focus on the 'Client Trends' chart area on the Dashboard page
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Client Trends' chart is displayed for <selected_office> showing the series 'New Clients' and 'Closed Clients' and the legend contains 'New Clients' and 'Closed Clients'

test_cases.test_case: Summary cards display numeric totals when data exists for the selected office
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
Dashboard page is open with <selected_office> selected that has dashboard data
steps: 1. Scroll to the summary cards below the 'Client Trends' chart
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Amount Pending / Disbursed' card displays a numeric total for <selected_office> and the 'Amount Collected' card displays a numeric total for <selected_office>

test_cases.test_case: Summary cards show 'No Data' when no information is available for the selected office
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
Dashboard page is open with <selected_office> selected that has no dashboard data
steps: 1. Scroll to the summary cards below the 'Client Trends' chart
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Amount Pending / Disbursed' card displays 'No Data' and the 'Amount Collected' card displays 'No Data'

test_cases.test_case: Search Activity field accepts input
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
Dashboard page is open
steps: 1. Enter <search term> in the Search Activity field
2. Press Enter or trigger the search action
test_cases.expected_result: The Search Activity field contains <search term>

test_cases.test_case: Click Dashboard button when not on Home page (precondition not met)
preconditions: User is on <any page other than Home page>
steps: 1. Navigate to <any page other than Home page>
2. Attempt to click the 'Dashboard' button
test_cases.expected_result: 'Dashboard' button is not visible (or is disabled) when the user is not on the Home page; clicking is not possible and no navigation occurs — the Dashboard page is not opened

test_cases.test_case: Very long search string (200+ chars) in Search Activity
preconditions: User is on Home page
steps: 1. Click the Dashboard button on the Home page
2. Focus the Search Activity field on the Dashboard page
3. Enter a very long string (200+ characters) in the Search Activity field
4. Submit the search (press Enter or click the search action)
test_cases.expected_result: Search succeeds in accepting the long input: the Search Activity field displays the entered text (if the UI truncates input it visibly shows the truncated value in the field); no application error or crash occurs. The results area updates: either matching activity is shown or, if no data matches for the selected office, the summary cards below display the visible text 'No Data'.

test_cases.test_case: Special characters and emoji in Search Activity
preconditions: User is on Home page
steps: 1. Click the Dashboard button on the Home page
2. Focus the Search Activity field on the Dashboard page
3. Enter a search string containing special characters and emoji (e.g., punctuation, symbols, emoji, non-Latin unicode) in the Search Activity field
4. Submit the search (press Enter or click the search action)
test_cases.expected_result: Search input accepts and displays the entered special characters/emoji; no inline validation error is shown. The Dashboard does not break visually: the Client Trends chart area and legend remain visible and the summary cards update (showing matched results or 'No Data' if there are no matches). There is no rendering corruption or JavaScript error visible to the user.

test_cases.test_case: Rapidly switch selected office to one with no data and verify 'No Data' state
preconditions: User is on Home page
There exists at least one office with data and at least one office with no data for the user
steps: 1. Click the Dashboard button on the Home page
2. Open the office selector control bound to the Dashboard (selected_office)
3. Select an office that has data (office A)
4. Immediately (within ~1 second) select an office that has no data (office B)
5. Observe the Client Trends chart and the two summary cards below the chart
test_cases.expected_result: Selecting the office with no data succeeds: the Client Trends chart displays its empty state (no data series plotted) and both summary cards visibly display 'No Data'. No stale data from the previously selected office remains visible in the chart or summary cards, and the chart legend remains present. No UI errors or crashes occur during or after the rapid switch.

test_cases.test_case: Open the global search input from the top toolbar
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar
test_cases.expected_result: Search input field opens inline (Search_Input is visible) and the input is focused

test_cases.test_case: Enter a partial term and see grouped results across entity types
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar
2. Enter <partial term> in the Search input
test_cases.expected_result: Results dropdown appears showing groups 'Clients', 'Groups', 'Loans', and 'Savings accounts'. Each visible item in groups displays entity name, identifier, and status; only items matching <partial term> are shown and unrelated items are not visible

test_cases.test_case: Search is case-insensitive and supports partial matching
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar
2. Enter <mixed-case partial term> in the Search input
test_cases.expected_result: Results dropdown displays items whose names or identifiers match <mixed-case partial term> regardless of letter case; matching items are shown in their respective entity groups

test_cases.test_case: Select a Client result from the dropdown and navigate to Client detail

steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar
2. Enter <search term that matches a client> in the Search input
3. In the Clients group of the results dropdown, click Select on a matching item
test_cases.expected_result: Browser navigates to {entity_type} detail page

test_cases.test_case: Select a Group result from the dropdown and navigate to Group detail
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar
2. Enter <search term that matches a group> in the Search input
3. In the Groups group of the results dropdown, click Select on a matching item
test_cases.expected_result: Browser navigates to {entity_type} detail page

test_cases.test_case: Select a Loan result from the dropdown and navigate to Loan detail
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar
2. Enter <search term that matches a loan> in the Search input
3. In the Loans group of the results dropdown, click Select on a matching item
test_cases.expected_result: Browser navigates to {entity_type} detail page

test_cases.test_case: Select a Savings account result from the dropdown and navigate to Savings account detail
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar
2. Enter <search term that matches a savings account> in the Search input
3. In the Savings accounts group of the results dropdown, click Select on a matching item
test_cases.expected_result: Browser navigates to {entity_type} detail page

test_cases.test_case: Enter a term with no matches and see the 'No results found' message
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar
2. Enter <search term with no matches> in the Search input
test_cases.expected_result: 'No results found' is displayed in the results dropdown

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot open global search
preconditions: User is not logged in
steps: 1. Ensure the user session is logged out
2. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar
test_cases.expected_result: Search input does not open; user is redirected to the Login page and the Login screen is displayed; the Search_Input field and results dropdown are not shown (global search is not accessible before login).

test_cases.test_case: Search shows 'No results found' for a query with no matches
preconditions: User is logged in
steps: 1. Log in as <any valid user>
2. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar
3. Type <query that matches no Clients, Groups, Loans, or Savings accounts> into the Search_Input
4. Wait for the results dropdown to populate
test_cases.expected_result: Results dropdown displays the exact message 'No results found'; no result rows are shown for any entity type; no navigation to a detail page occurs.

test_cases.test_case: Very long search query (200+ characters)
preconditions: user must be logged in
Search Icon is visible in the top toolbar
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon
2. Enter <very long search string (200+ characters)> into Search_Input
test_cases.expected_result: Search executes and the results dropdown updates (succeeds). If matches exist they are displayed grouped by entity type with entity_name, identifier, and status; if none exist the dropdown displays 'No results found'.

test_cases.test_case: Leading and trailing whitespace in query then selecting a result
preconditions: user must be logged in
Search Icon is visible in the top toolbar
There is at least one entity whose name matches the trimmed form of the query
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon
2. Enter <search term with leading and trailing whitespace> into Search_Input
3. Wait for the results dropdown to appear
4. Click 'Select' on the matching result item
test_cases.expected_result: Leading and trailing whitespace is ignored for matching; clicking 'Select' navigates to the correct entity detail page and the navigation succeeds. The clicked item's detail page is displayed (succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Query containing only special characters / emoji / unicode
preconditions: user must be logged in
Search Icon is visible in the top toolbar
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon
2. Enter <string of special characters and emoji/unicode> into Search_Input
test_cases.expected_result: The search input accepts special characters/emoji and the search executes (succeeds). If no matching entities exist the results dropdown displays 'No results found'. If matches exist they are displayed grouped by entity type with entity_name, identifier, and status.

test_cases.test_case: Select a result while dropdown is updating (race condition)
preconditions: user must be logged in
Search Icon is visible in the top toolbar
There are multiple entities that will match incremental updates for the partial query
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon
2. Enter <partial query expected to return multiple results> into Search_Input rapidly (character-by-character)
3. Immediately click 'Select' on a visible result item while the dropdown is still updating
4. Observe navigation
test_cases.expected_result: The click is applied to the specific item clicked and navigation to that item's detail page succeeds. No stale or incorrect entity detail is opened as a result of concurrent result updates (succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Open Create Client wizard from Clients page
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page
2. Click the 'Create Client' button in the top-right
test_cases.expected_result: opens Create Client wizard

test_cases.test_case: Submit Create Client wizard with required fields and an identifier
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page
2. Click 'Create Client' to open the Create Client wizard
3. On Step 1 (General Details) select <Office>, enter <First Name>, enter <Last Name>, select <Submitted On>
4. Click Next to proceed to Step 2, then click Next to proceed to Step 3
5. Click Next to reach Step 4 (Identifiers); click Add Row, enter <Document Type> and <Document Key>
6. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: creates client in Pending status

test_cases.test_case: Open Bulk Import page via Import Client link
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page
2. Click the 'Import Client' link at the top-right
test_cases.expected_result: opens Bulk Import page

steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page

test_cases.test_case: Upload completed client import file to start import job
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page
2. Click 'Import Client' to open the Bulk Import page
3. Click 'Upload Completed File' and select a <valid client import file>
4. Click the 'Upload' button
test_cases.expected_result: starts import job

test_cases.test_case: Download import result from Import History table
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
An import history entry exists for <existing import entry>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page
2. Click 'Import Client' to open the Bulk Import page
3. In the Import History table locate the row for <existing import entry>
4. Click the 'Download' action in that row
test_cases.expected_result: downloads import results for the selected import entry

test_cases.test_case: Search clients by a query that matches Name/Account/External ID/Mobile
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
Clients page is populated with multiple client rows
steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page
2. Enter <search query> in the Client_Search field and trigger search (press Enter or click Search)
test_cases.expected_result: Clients list displays only rows matching <search query> in Name, Account No., External ID, or Mobile Number; unrelated rows are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Filter clients by Status using the Status dropdown
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
Clients page is populated with clients in various statuses
steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page
2. Open the Status_Filter dropdown and select <Status>
test_cases.expected_result: Clients list displays only rows with Status = <Status>; unrelated rows are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Open Client Detail by clicking client Name in listing
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
Clients page shows a row for <target client>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page
2. Click the client Name link for <target client> in the table
test_cases.expected_result: navigates to Client Detail page for selected client

4. In the Activate dialog enter <Activation Date> that is on or after the client's <Submitted On> date

steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page

4. Modify an editable field (for example enter <new value> in the Mobile Number field)

steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page

steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page

Client Detail page is open for <target client> with at least one identifier present
steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page

test_cases.test_case: Create Client wizard: enter Address Details on Step 2 and verify on detail page after submit
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page
2. Click 'Create Client' to open the Create Client wizard
3. On Step 1 (General Details) select <Office>, enter <First Name>, enter <Last Name>, select <Submitted On>
4. Click Next to reach Step 2 (Address Details)

test_cases.test_case: Identifiers (Client Detail): Adding duplicate identifier entries is blocked

3. Enter <Document Key X> in the Document Key field of the new row
4. Click Save/Add to persist the row
5. Click Add to create another identifier row
6. Enter <Document Type A> in the Document Type field of the second row
7. Enter <Document Key X> in the Document Key field of the second row (same as the first row)
8. Click Save/Add to attempt to persist the duplicate row
test_cases.expected_result: Second add is blocked; the repeating group displays an inline error preventing the duplicate entry (duplicate identifier not allowed) and only one identifier row with Document Type = <Document Type A> and Document Key = <Document Key X> is present.

test_cases.test_case: Create Client wizard: rapid re-submission after redirect is blocked (no duplicate created)

3. Enter <Office> in the Office field
4. Enter <First Name> in the First Name field
5. Enter <Last Name> in the Last Name field
6. Enter <Submitted On> in the Submitted On field
7. Click Submit (creates client in Pending status and navigates to the created client's detail page)
8. On the client detail page, press the browser Back button
9. On the returned Create Client wizard page, click Submit again
test_cases.expected_result: Second submission attempt is blocked; only one client record is created with the entered details. Searching the Clients listing by the entered name returns a single result. No duplicate client is created.

test_cases.test_case: Create Client wizard: add then remove all identifiers then submit succeeds (no identifiers saved)

2. Enter <Document Type B> in the Document Type field
3. Enter <Document Key Y> in the Document Key field
4. Click Add/Save to persist the identifier row
5. Click Remove on the added identifier row to delete it
6. Navigate to the wizard Submit action and click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; client is created in Pending status. The created client's Identifiers list is empty (no identifiers saved).

test_cases.test_case: Reject (Pending): very long Reason (200+ chars) is handled
preconditions: A client exists in Pending status
steps: 1. Open the Clients page
2. Click the client Name to open the Client Detail page for that Pending client
3. Click the Reject action in the action bar
4. Enter a long text string (>= 200 characters) in the Reason field
5. Click Confirm/Reject
test_cases.expected_result: Reject action succeeds; client status changes to Rejected and the saved Reason is displayed on the client detail page. If the system truncates long reasons, a truncation indicator (e.g., ellipsis with 'show more') is displayed next to the saved text. No unhandled UI error is shown.

test_cases.test_case: State violation — Close should not be available when group is Closed

User is logged in and on that Group's Detail page
steps: 1. Open the Group Detail page for a group whose Status is Closed
2. Inspect the Group Action Bar for a Close action/button
3. Attempt to click Close if it is present
test_cases.expected_result: Close action/button is not present in the action bar for a Closed group; no close action occurs and the group's status remains Closed (the action is blocked/not available)

test_cases.test_case: Collection Sheet — enter non-numeric value into numeric amount field

3. Attempt to save/submit the collection sheet (or move focus away from the edited cell)
test_cases.expected_result: The Loan_Repayment_Amount field displays an inline validation error indicating a numeric value is required; the collection sheet cannot be saved/submitted and the invalid value is not accepted

test_cases.test_case: Submit Create Group with Selected_Clients added then removed (repeating_group min=0 boundary)

User can open Create Group form
steps: 1. Click Create New Group
2. Enter a valid value in Name
3. Select a value in Office
4. Enter a value in Submitted_On (valid date)

6. In Selected_Clients, remove the added client so the repeating group is empty
7. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; group is created and the app navigates to the Group Detail page; Members tab (or Members list) shows no members for the newly created group (creation with zero Selected_Clients succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Name field with leading and trailing whitespace is trimmed on save

3. Enter a valid value in Submitted_On
4. Click Submit

test_cases.test_case: Submitted_On set to today's date is accepted

steps: 1. Enter a valid Name
2. Select a value in Office
3. Set Submitted_On to today's date
4. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; Group Detail page shows Submitted On equal to today's date exactly as entered (submission with today's date succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Submitted_On set to a far-future date (edge of plausibility) is persisted

steps: 1. Enter a valid Name
2. Select a value in Office
3. Set Submitted_On to a far-future date (e.g., one year from today)
4. Click Submit

test_cases.test_case: Rapid consecutive transitions: Activate a Pending group then immediately Close it
preconditions: A group exists with status Pending
User is on that group's Group Detail page and can see action bar
steps: 1. On Group Detail page with status Pending, click Activate
2. Immediately after the Activate action completes, click Close
test_cases.expected_result: Both state transitions succeed in sequence; after the first action the status changes to Active, and after the second action the status changes to Closed and the status chip displays Closed (rapid consecutive transitions succeed).

test_cases.test_case: Activate action is not present when group is Active (availability boundary)
preconditions: A group exists with status Active
User is on that group's Group Detail page
steps: 1. Observe the Group Action Bar on a group whose status is Active
test_cases.expected_result: The Activate action/button is not visible in the action bar when status is Active; attempting to locate or click Activate via the visible UI is not possible (action availability correctly blocked for this state).

test_cases.test_case: Collection Sheet numeric fields accept values with excessive decimal places and display rounded persisted value

3. Save or submit the collection sheet (as per UI flow)
test_cases.expected_result: Submission succeeds; the Collection Sheet displays the saved numeric values rounded to the system's stored/display precision (entered excessive decimal places are rounded on save and the rounded values are visible in the sheet), and no generic validation error blocks the save.

test_cases.test_case: Back-button after successful Create Group does not create a duplicate on re-submit

test_cases.expected_result: Second submission is blocked or prevented by the UI; only one group record is created. After step 5, the system does not create a duplicate group (only the original group exists in Groups list or the UI shows that a duplicate submission was prevented).

test_cases.expected_result: Either the creation succeeds and the Share Product detail view displays the full Description (no truncation) OR the form prevents submission with a visible inline message or helper indicating truncation/maximum length and the Description input visibly shows the truncated/text-limited value; no silent data loss occurs and an explicit UI indicator is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Leading/trailing whitespace in Short Name is handled
preconditions: User is on Share Products page
+ Create Share Product wizard is opened
steps: 1. Click + Create Share Product
2. In Step 1 (Details), enter valid values for required fields Product Name and Description
3. In Short Name, enter a value that includes leading and trailing spaces (e.g., '  short  ')
4. Click Next until the Create Share Product button is visible
5. Click Create Share Product
test_cases.expected_result: Form submission either succeeds and the Share Product detail view displays Short Name with leading/trailing whitespace removed (trimmed), OR the form shows an inline validation message preventing submission; the UI clearly shows whether trimming occurred (detail view shows trimmed value) or an error is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to jump to a later wizard step without completing earlier steps
preconditions: User is on Share Products page
+ Create Share Product wizard is opened
steps: 1. Click + Create Share Product
2. From Step 1 (Details), attempt to click the Step 4 (Settings) tab/header directly
3. Observe which step becomes active
test_cases.expected_result: If the wizard enforces sequential completion, navigation is blocked and Step 1 remains active with an inline hint or visual indicator that earlier required fields must be completed; if the wizard allows free navigation, Step 4 becomes active but no implicit validation errors are shown for Step 1 fields until final submission. The UI must either block the jump (Step 1 stays active) or allow the jump (Step 4 visible) — the behavior is explicit and visible.

test_cases.test_case: Add then remove all Market Price rows and submit
preconditions: User is on Share Products page
+ Create Share Product wizard is opened
Completed Steps 1-4 with valid values for required fields
steps: 1. Click + Create Share Product
2. Complete Steps 1-4 with valid inputs
3. Navigate to Step 5 (Market Price)
4. Click Add Row to add one Market Price row
5. In the new row enter a valid From Date
6. Enter a valid Share Value in that row
7. Click the row's Remove Row action to remove the only Market Price row
8. Click Next until the Create Share Product button is visible
9. Click Create Share Product
test_cases.expected_result: If the system does not require any Market Price rows, the form submission succeeds and the Share Product is created; if the system requires at least one Market Price row, submission is blocked and a visible inline error is shown on Step 5 (e.g., near Market Price table) indicating that at least one row is required. No silent failures occur.

test_cases.test_case: Rapidly toggle Accounting Method to reveal/hide GL fields
preconditions: User is on Share Products page
+ Create Share Product wizard is opened
Reached Step 7 (Accounting)
steps: 1. In Step 7, click Accounting Method = Cash-based
2. Immediately (within one second) click Accounting Method = None
3. Immediately click Accounting Method = Cash-based again
4. Observe the GL dropdown fields area for duplication, retained values, and any visible errors
test_cases.expected_result: When toggling to Cash-based the GL dropdown fields become visible; rapid toggling back to None hides them; toggling back to Cash-based re-displays the GL fields without creating duplicate UI elements or causing JS/render errors. Either previously entered GL values are preserved or cleared explicitly, but the UI remains consistent and no duplicate fields or console-level error messages are visible to the user.

test_cases.test_case: High-precision Nominal/Unit Price input vs Decimal Places setting
preconditions: User is on Share Products page
+ Create Share Product wizard is opened
steps: 1. Click + Create Share Product
2. In Step 2 (Currency), set Decimal Places to a specific small integer value (e.g., set field to a value)
3. Click Next to reach Step 3 (Terms)
4. In Nominal/Unit Price enter a number with more decimal places than the Decimal Places value set in Step 2
5. Click Next until the Create Share Product button is visible
6. Click Create Share Product
test_cases.expected_result: Either the system rounds the entered Nominal/Unit Price to the configured Decimal Places and the Share Product detail view shows the rounded value, or the form blocks submission with a clear inline error indicating the decimal precision constraint. The UI must visibly show rounding or an explicit validation message.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to click a product link behind the open Create wizard (background click blocking)
preconditions: User is on Share Products page with at least one existing product in the table
+ Create Share Product wizard is opened
steps: 1. Click + Create Share Product
2. While the wizard modal/stepper is open, attempt to click a Product Name link in the underlying Share Products table
3. Observe whether the Product Details view opens or the click is ignored and the wizard remains active
test_cases.expected_result: Background product link clicks are blocked while the Create wizard is open: the Product Details view does not open, the wizard remains active and focused, and a visible overlay or modal state prevents navigation. No unexpected navigation occurs.

test_cases.test_case: Very long Charge Name with special/unicode characters

The UI must visibly indicate which of the two behaviors occurred.

test_cases.test_case: Charge Name with leading and trailing whitespace is trimmed on save

Click '+ Create Charge' to open Create_Charge_Form

2. In Charge_Applies_To select 'Client'

4. In Charge_Calculation_Type select 'Flat'
5. In Amount enter a valid numeric value
6. Click 'Submit'

test_cases.test_case: Amount with excessive decimal precision
preconditions: Open the Charges page
Click '+ Create Charge' to open Create_Charge_Form
steps: 1. In Charge_Name enter a valid name
2. In Charge_Applies_To select 'Shares'
3. In Currency select a value
4. In Charge_Calculation_Type select 'Percentage of Amount'
5. In Amount enter a numeric value with many decimal places (more decimals than typical display precision)
6. Click 'Submit'
test_cases.expected_result: Either the form submission succeeds and the Charge Detail displays the saved Amount rounded to the system's supported decimal precision, OR submission is blocked and an inline error is shown indicating unacceptable decimal precision. The visible behavior must indicate whether rounding or an error occurred.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid re-submission / browser back duplicate prevention after successful create
preconditions: Open the Charges page
Click '+ Create Charge' to open Create_Charge_Form
steps: 1. Fill all required fields with valid values and click 'Submit' to create a charge
2. Wait for navigation to the Charge Detail page
3. Use the browser Back button to return to the Create_Charge_Form
4. Immediately click 'Submit' again without changing fields
test_cases.expected_result: Second submission attempt is blocked (or ignored); only one charge record is created. The Charges table or detail view shows a single created charge (no duplicate) and a visible message or lack of duplicate indicates the second submission was prevented.

test_cases.test_case: Charge_Time_Type validity when Charge_Applies_To changes (Loan -> Savings Account)
preconditions: Open the Charges page
Click '+ Create Charge' to open Create_Charge_Form
steps: 1. In Charge_Applies_To select 'Loan'
2. In Charge_Time_Type select a Loan-only option (e.g. 'Disbursement')
3. Change Charge_Applies_To to 'Savings Account'
4. Observe the Charge_Time_Type field
5. Attempt to click 'Submit' without changing Charge_Time_Type
test_cases.expected_result: When Charge_Applies_To is changed such that the previously selected Charge_Time_Type is not valid, the Charge_Time_Type selection is cleared (no invalid option remains) and submission is blocked until a valid Charge_Time_Type for 'Savings Account' is selected. Submission succeeds only after selecting a valid Charge_Time_Type for the new applies-to value.

test_cases.test_case: Charge_Time_Type validity when Charge_Applies_To changes (Savings Account -> Loan)
preconditions: Open the Charges page
Click '+ Create Charge' to open Create_Charge_Form
steps: 1. In Charge_Applies_To select 'Savings Account'
2. In Charge_Time_Type select a Savings-only option (e.g. 'Savings Activation')
3. Change Charge_Applies_To to 'Loan'
4. Observe the Charge_Time_Type field
5. Attempt to click 'Submit' without changing Charge_Time_Type
test_cases.expected_result: When Charge_Applies_To is changed such that the previously selected Charge_Time_Type is not valid, the Charge_Time_Type selection is cleared (no invalid option remains) and submission is blocked until a valid Charge_Time_Type for 'Loan' is selected. Submission succeeds only after selecting a valid Charge_Time_Type for the new applies-to value.

test_cases.test_case: Create a delinquency bucket with multiple ranges and link loan products

test_cases.test_case: Create delinquency range with blank Maximum Age Days (applies to all days beyond minimum)

test_cases.test_case: Create delinquency range where Minimum Age Days equals Maximum Age Days (single-day range)

test_cases.test_case: Enter a decimal (fractional) value in a range's Minimum Age Days within Create Delinquency Bucket repeating group

test_cases.test_case: Add multiple ranges in repeating group then remove all entries, submit with zero ranges

4. Click Add Range
5. In the second range item, enter valid Minimum Age Days and Maximum Age Days
6. Remove the first range item (click the range item Remove/Delete control)
7. Remove the second range item (click the range item Remove/Delete control) so no ranges remain
8. Click the Create button
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully (no minimum count specified for ranges); the new bucket appears in the Delinquency Buckets table with an empty Associated Ranges cell and the bucket detail page shows zero associated ranges — succeeds.

No loan is created; page remains on the wizard.

preconditions: A loan exists in 'Written Off' status and the Loan Detail page is open
steps: 1. Inspect the action bar on the Loan Detail page for the loan in 'Written Off' status
test_cases.expected_result: No action buttons are visible in the action bar for loans in 'Written Off' status (action bar is empty); no actions can be performed from this state.

test_cases.test_case: Submit loan with Principal = selected product.min (boundary pass)
preconditions: A client exists and is visible on Client Detail page
A loan product is available with defined min and max Principal values
steps: 1. From Client Detail page, click New Loan Application
2. In Product Name dropdown, select the product with known min and max Principal
3. In Principal field, enter <selected product.min>
4. In Interest Rate field, enter a value within <selected product.min> and <selected product.max>
5. In Number of Repayments, enter a valid number
6. In Repaid Every, enter a valid frequency number
7. In Repaid Unit dropdown, select a valid unit
8. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; loan is created with status 'Submitted and Pending Approval' and the loan detail header displays the Submitted and Pending Approval status badge (succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to submit loan with Principal = one unit below selected product.min (boundary fail)
preconditions: A client exists and is visible on Client Detail page
A loan product is available with defined min and max Principal values
steps: 1. From Client Detail page, click New Loan Application
2. In Product Name dropdown, select the product with known min and max Principal
3. In Principal field, enter <one unit below selected product.min>
4. In Interest Rate field, enter a value within <selected product.min> and <selected product.max>
5. In Number of Repayments, enter a valid number
6. In Repaid Every, enter a valid frequency number
7. In Repaid Unit dropdown, select a valid unit
8. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is blocked and the Principal field displays an inline error indicating the value is below the minimum allowed (is blocked / error shown).

test_cases.test_case: Submit loan with Interest Rate = selected product.max (boundary pass)
preconditions: A client exists and is visible on Client Detail page
A loan product is available with defined min and max Interest Rate values
steps: 1. From Client Detail page, click New Loan Application
2. In Product Name dropdown, select the product with known min and max Interest Rate
3. In Interest Rate field, enter <selected product.max>
4. In Principal field, enter a value within <selected product.min> and <selected product.max>
5. Fill remaining required fields in Details step (Number of Repayments, Repaid Every, Repaid Unit)
6. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; loan is created with status 'Submitted and Pending Approval' and the Interest Rate shown on the loan detail page equals <selected product.max> (succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to submit loan with Interest Rate = one unit above selected product.max (boundary fail)
preconditions: A client exists and is visible on Client Detail page
A loan product is available with defined min and max Interest Rate values
steps: 1. From Client Detail page, click New Loan Application
2. In Product Name dropdown, select the product with known min and max Interest Rate
3. In Interest Rate field, enter <one unit above selected product.max>
4. In Principal field, enter a value within <selected product.min> and <selected product.max>
5. Fill remaining required fields in Details step (Number of Repayments, Repaid Every, Repaid Unit)
6. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is blocked and the Interest Rate field displays an inline error indicating the value is above the maximum allowed (is blocked / error shown).

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to navigate to Charges step before completing required fields in Details (wizard step-skip block)
preconditions: User is on Client Detail page
New Loan Application wizard opens
steps: 1. From Client Detail page, click New Loan Application
2. In the wizard, attempt to click the tab or step heading for 'Charges' (Step 3) without filling required fields in Step 1 (Details)
3. Observe UI response
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation to the 'Charges' step is blocked; the wizard keeps the Details step active and a visible prompt or inline indicator highlights the required fields that must be completed first (is blocked / error shown).

test_cases.test_case: Add a Charge row then remove all Charge rows and submit (repeating group add/remove edge)
preconditions: User has opened New Loan Application wizard and reached Charges step
steps: 1. In Charges step, click Add Charge
2. For the new Charge row, fill required Charge_Name and Amount fields with valid values
3. Click the remove/delete control for that Charge row to remove it
4. Confirm the Charges repeating_group shows zero rows
5. Navigate back to Details (or ensure required fields in Details are filled)
6. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; loan is created with status 'Submitted and Pending Approval' despite there being zero charge rows since Charges are optional (succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: External_ID with leading and trailing whitespace is trimmed on save
preconditions: User has opened New Loan Application wizard and is on Details step
steps: 1. In External_ID field, enter a value with visible leading and trailing whitespace (e.g., ' <value> ')
2. Fill all required fields in Details (Product, Principal within bounds, Number of Repayments, Repaid Every, Repaid Unit, Interest Rate within bounds)
3. Click Submit
4. Open the created loan's detail page
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; the External_ID displayed on the loan detail page is shown without leading or trailing whitespace (leading/trailing whitespace is trimmed) (succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of Approve on a Submitted application (concurrent state transition)
preconditions: A loan exists in 'Submitted and Pending Approval' status and is open in Loan Detail page
steps: 1. On the loan detail page, locate the Approve action in the state action bar
2. Click Approve once
3. Immediately (before the UI visibly updates the status), click the Approve action again
4. Observe the UI status and available actions
test_cases.expected_result: Only one approval is processed; the second click is blocked and an inline message or disabled state prevents a duplicate approval. The loan status changes to 'Approved' and the Approve action is no longer present (is blocked / error shown for the duplicate).

test_cases.test_case: Enter Interest Rate with higher-than-supported decimal precision and verify stored precision
preconditions: A client exists and is visible on Client Detail page
A loan product is available with defined min and max Interest Rate values
steps: 1. From Client Detail page, click New Loan Application
2. In Product Name dropdown, select the product with known min and max Interest Rate
3. In Interest Rate field, enter a value with more decimal places than typical field display precision (high-precision decimal)
4. Fill remaining required fields in Details (Principal within bounds, Number of Repayments, Repaid Every, Repaid Unit)
5. Click Submit
6. Open the created loan's detail page and view the stored Interest Rate value
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; the Interest Rate displayed on the loan detail page is rounded/truncated to the system-supported decimal precision (visible rounded value on detail page) (succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Withdraw exactly equal to available balance succeeds

test_cases.test_case: Attempt Activate immediately after Approve (rapid consecutive state transitions)
preconditions: A Share Account exists in 'Submitted and Pending Approval' status (AST: state 'Submitted and Pending Approval' available)

Activate action is only available in Approved state (AST: state 'Approved' available actions include Activate)

2. Click the Approve action
3. Immediately (without waiting for page refresh or status confirmation) attempt to click Activate
test_cases.expected_result: Second action is blocked / error shown: Activate does not execute because the account's status has not yet reached 'Approved' in the UI; UI shows Activate is not available or a visible message indicates the action cannot be performed until approval finalizes (no unintended account state transition occurs)

test_cases.test_case: Previously-selected Parent_Account becomes invalid when Account_Type is changed after selection

test_cases.test_case: Transaction_Date = one day after latest closing date (boundary pass)

steps: 1. Click + Add Journal Entry

9. Click Submit

test_cases.test_case: Entry lines totals differ by one smallest unit (off-by-one) — submission blocked
preconditions: User has permission to create journal entries
Selected Office has a latest closing date earlier than the Transaction_Date to be used
steps: 1. Click + Add Journal Entry

9. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is blocked / error shown; a running totals difference indicator and an inline validation message indicate total_debits must equal total_credits. The journal entry is not created.

test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; settlement succeeds and a new transaction appears in the Cashier Transactions list with Date equal to <today> (visible in the transactions table).

test_cases.test_case: Rapid re-submission after Create Teller (browser back then submit)
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Tellers page
steps: 1. Click the '+ Create Teller' button
2. Enter <Office> in the Office field
3. Enter <Teller Name> in the Teller Name field
4. Enter <today> in the Start Date field
5. Click Submit and wait for the success redirect/confirmation
6. Use the browser back button to return to the Create Teller form
7. Click Submit again without changing fields
test_cases.expected_result: Second submission is blocked or prevented from creating a duplicate; only one new teller record appears in the Tellers table (the UI shows either the Create action is disabled or an error/notification is shown preventing duplicate creation).

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to User detail by clicking Username in Users table
preconditions: User logged in as <Administrator>
Users page is open and contains an existing user with Username <existing username>
steps: 1. On the Users page, locate the row with Username <existing username>
2. Click the Username link for <existing username>
test_cases.expected_result: The User_Detail_Page for <existing username> is displayed (the detail view for the clicked Username is visible)

test_cases.test_case: Create a new user using the Create User form (happy path including optional fields)
preconditions: User logged in as <Administrator>
Users page is open
Username <new username> is not already present in the Users table
steps: 1. Click the '+ Create User' button on the Users page to open the Create User form
2. Enter <new username> in the Username field
3. Enter <first name> in the First Name field
4. Enter <last name> in the Last Name field
5. Enter <email address> in the Email field
6. Enter or select <office> in the Office field
7. Select <staff record> from the Staff dropdown (optional link to staff)
8. Enter <valid password> in the Password field (meets password policy)
9. Enter the same <valid password> in the Repeat Password field
10. Select one or more Roles from the Roles checkboxes
11. Optionally check 'Override Password Expiry Policy' and/or 'Send Password to Email' as desired
12. Click the Submit button on the Create User form
test_cases.expected_result: A new row appears in the Users table with the entered Username <new username> and Email <email address>

test_cases.test_case: Create a role and open the Role Permissions page

Roles page is open

2. Enter <role name> in the Role Name field
3. Enter <description> in the Description field
4. Click the Submit button on the Create Role form
test_cases.expected_result: The Role Permissions page is displayed for the new role with a permissions matrix showing categories User Management, Portfolio, Organization, Accounting, Reports, and Other_Permissions; each category shows its permission checkboxes

test_cases.test_case: Enable a permission checkbox on the Role Permissions page

2. Check the checkbox for <specific permission> under User Management
test_cases.expected_result: The checkbox for <specific permission> is checked on the Role Permissions page for <role name>

test_cases.test_case: Leave required text field (Username) blank and submit Create User form

3. Enter <valid last name> in Last Name
4. Enter <valid email> in Email
5. Enter <valid office> in Office
6. Enter <valid password meeting policy> in Password
7. Enter the same <valid password meeting policy> in Repeat Password

test_cases.test_case: Create Role with very long Description (200+ chars)
preconditions: User is logged in with permission to create roles
Roles page is open
steps: 1. Click "+ Create Role"
2. Enter a valid value in the Role Name field
3. Enter a very long string (200+ characters) in the Description field
4. Click the form submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Either the role is created and the saved Role Description in the Roles table/detail is shown truncated to the system maximum (succeeds) OR the submit is blocked and an inline error is shown on Description indicating the description exceeds the maximum length (is blocked / error shown).

test_cases.test_case: Create User with special/unicode characters (emoji, non-ASCII) in Username and Email local-part
preconditions: User is logged in with permission to create users
Users page is open
steps: 1. Click "+ Create User"
2. Enter a Username containing emoji and other non-ASCII characters
3. Enter an Email that includes non-ASCII characters or uncommon special characters in the local-part
4. Enter required First Name
5. Enter required Last Name
6. Enter required Office
7. Enter Password that meets the password policy
8. Enter the same value into Repeat Password
9. Click the form submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Either the user is created and the Users table/detail displays the Username and Email exactly as entered (succeeds) OR the submit is blocked with an inline validation error on Email and/or Username indicating invalid format or unsupported characters (is blocked / error shown).

test_cases.test_case: Rapid re-submission after successful Create User (uniqueness race via browser Back)
preconditions: User is logged in with permission to create users
No existing user with the chosen Username
steps: 1. Click "+ Create User"
2. Enter a unique Username
3. Enter required First Name
4. Enter required Last Name
5. Enter a valid Email
6. Enter required Office
7. Enter Password that meets the password policy
8. Enter the same value into Repeat Password
9. Click the form submit button
10. On successful navigation to the User_Detail_Page, click the browser Back button
11. On the Create_User_Form that appears, click the form submit button again without changing the Username
test_cases.expected_result: The second submission attempt is blocked; the Username field displays an inline error indicating the username is already taken (is blocked / error shown). Only one user record with that Username exists in the Users table (succeeds that duplicate creation is prevented).

test_cases.test_case: Password/Repeat Password mismatch caused by trailing/leading whitespace
preconditions: User is logged in with permission to create users
Users page is open
steps: 1. Click "+ Create User"
2. Enter a unique Username
3. Enter required First Name
4. Enter required Last Name
5. Enter a valid Email
6. Enter required Office
7. Enter a Password that meets the password policy with a trailing space appended
8. Enter the same password into Repeat Password but omit the trailing space
9. Click the form submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is blocked; the Repeat Password (or Password) field displays a visible validation error indicating the passwords do not match (is blocked / error shown).

test_cases.test_case: Date Range: invalid date format blocks Run Report
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Reports page
Report Parameters form is open for a selected report (Report_Parameters_Form rendered)
steps: 1. Click the <Report Name> link in the All tab to open the Report Parameters form
2. Enter <invalid date format> into the Date Range field (e.g. non-date text or malformed date)
3. Click the Run Report button
test_cases.expected_result: Date Range field displays an inline validation error indicating the date format is invalid; the Run Report action is blocked and Report_Results_Table is NOT rendered (no report results appear).

test_cases.test_case: Date Range: start date later than end date blocks Run Report
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Reports page
Report Parameters form is open for a selected report (Report_Parameters_Form rendered)
steps: 1. Click the <Report Name> link in the All tab to open the Report Parameters form
2. Enter <start date later than end date> into the Date Range field (start > end)
3. Click the Run Report button
test_cases.expected_result: Date Range field displays an inline validation error indicating the start date is after the end date; the Run Report action is blocked and Report_Results_Table is NOT rendered (no report results appear).

test_cases.test_case: Export to Excel not available before a report is run
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Reports page
No report has been run in the current view (Report_Results_Table is NOT rendered)
steps: 1. Ensure the Reports page is visible and no report has been executed (Report_Results_Table not present)
2. Attempt to click the Export to Excel control
test_cases.expected_result: Export to Excel control is not visible or is disabled until a report is generated; clicking it does not download a file and no export occurs; user remains on the Reports page with no Report_Results_Table rendered.

test_cases.test_case: View On Screen option unavailable before running a report
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Reports page
No report has been run in the current view (Report_Results_Table is NOT rendered)
steps: 1. Ensure the Reports page is visible and no report has been executed (Report_Results_Table not present)
2. Attempt to click the View On Screen output option
test_cases.expected_result: View On Screen option is not visible or is disabled until a report is generated; clicking it has no effect and no report is displayed on screen; Report_Results_Table remains not rendered.

test_cases.test_case: Date Range: only start date provided (incomplete range) blocks Run Report
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Reports page
Report Parameters form is open for a selected report (Report_Parameters_Form rendered)
steps: 1. Click the <Report Name> link in the All tab to open the Report Parameters form
2. Enter <only a start date provided> into the Date Range field (leave end date blank)
3. Click the Run Report button
test_cases.expected_result: Date Range field displays an inline validation error indicating a complete date range (both start and end) is required; the Run Report action is blocked and Report_Results_Table is NOT rendered (no report results appear).

test_cases.test_case: Single-day Date Range (start = end = <today>) when running a report
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Reports page
All tab is visible
steps: 1. Click the Name link for any report in the All tab
2. In the opened Report Parameters Form, set Date Range start to <today>
3. Set Date Range end to <today>
4. Click Run Report
test_cases.expected_result: Form submission succeeds; Report_Results_Table renders successfully and displays results for the single-day range; pagination and output option controls are visible.

test_cases.test_case: Single-day Date Range in the past (start = end = <yesterday>)
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Reports page
All tab is visible
steps: 1. Click the Name link for any report in the All tab
2. In the opened Report Parameters Form, set Date Range start to <yesterday>
3. Set Date Range end to <yesterday>
4. Click Run Report
test_cases.expected_result: Form submission succeeds; Report_Results_Table renders successfully and displays results (if any) for the specified past single-day range; pagination and output option controls are visible.

test_cases.test_case: Far-future Date Range yields zero rows in results table
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Reports page
All tab is visible
steps: 1. Click the Name link for any report in the All tab
2. In the opened Report Parameters Form, set Date Range start to a <far future date>
3. Set Date Range end to a <far future date> (>= start)
4. Click Run Report
test_cases.expected_result: Form submission succeeds; Report_Results_Table renders and displays zero data rows (empty state) for the selected date range; pagination shows no data pages and output option controls remain available but indicate no data to display.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of Run Report button (prevent duplicate runs)
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Reports page
All tab is visible
Report Parameters Form is open for a chosen report
steps: 1. Set Date Range start to <today> in the Report Parameters Form
2. Set Date Range end to <today>
3. Click Run Report
4. Immediately click Run Report again (within a rapid double-click)
5. Observe the UI until the report results render or an error appears
test_cases.expected_result: Second submission attempt is blocked/ignored; only one Report_Results_Table render occurs for the request; the Run Report control shows a transient loading state and does not cause duplicate result tables or duplicate export jobs.

test_cases.test_case: Export to Excel when results table contains zero data rows (far-future date range)
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Reports page
All tab is visible
A report has been run and Report_Results_Table is displayed with zero data rows
steps: 1. In Report_Results_Table, click the Export to Excel output option
2. Wait for the file download to be initiated/completed
test_cases.expected_result: Export to Excel succeeds; a file download is initiated and the downloaded Excel file contains the report header row(s) and zero data rows (no data records).

test_cases.test_case: Run report with all parameter fields left blank (no filters)
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Reports page
All tab is visible
steps: 1. Click the Name link for any report in the All tab
2. Do not change any dropdowns or the Date Range (leave all parameters blank)
3. Click Run Report
test_cases.expected_result: Form submission succeeds; Report_Results_Table renders with results for the unfiltered report; pagination controls are present (if result count exceeds page size) and output option controls are visible.

test_cases.test_case: Process Account Transfer with sufficient balance
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
Source account <From_Account> has available balance >= <transfer amount>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Account Transfers page
2. Select <From_Office> in From Office
3. Select <From_Client> in From Client
4. Select <From_Account_Type> in From Account Type
5. Select <From_Account> in From Account
6. Select <To_Office> in To Office
7. Select <To_Client> in To Client
8. Select <To_Account_Type> in To Account Type
9. Select <To_Account> in To Account
10. Enter <transfer amount> in Transfer Amount (<= available balance)
11. Enter <transfer date> in Transfer Date
12. Enter <description> in Description (optional)
13. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: A success notification is shown with text: processes transfer; debits source account and credits destination account

test_cases.test_case: Create a new Standing Instruction via top action
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Standing Instructions page
2. Click + Create Standing Instruction
3. In the Create Standing Instruction form, enter <Name> in Name (required)
4. Select <From_Account> in From Account (optional)
5. Select <To_Account> in To Account (optional)
6. Select <Instruction_Type> in Instruction Type
7. If Instruction Type is Fixed, enter <Amount> in Amount (optional)
8. Enter <validity from date> in Validity From (optional) and <validity till date> in Validity Till (optional)
9. Select <Recurrence_Type> in Recurrence Type (optional) and fill Recurrence Frequency/Interval as <values> if Periodic
10. Click Create
test_cases.expected_result: A success notification is shown with text: creates standing instruction; a new row appears in the Standing Instructions table with Name = <Name>

test_cases.expected_result: The row for <Name> shows Status = 'Active' in the Status column (changes status to Active)

test_cases.test_case: Disable an Active Standing Instruction from the listing

steps: 1. Navigate to the Standing Instructions page

test_cases.test_case: Delete a Standing Instruction from the listing
